ground_truth.title: 9 Best Online Course Selling Platforms For Creators (2026) [I Tested All Of Them]
ground_truth.author: Jeff Cobb
ground_truth.main_content:
TL;DR: What Is The Best Platform To Sell Courses? My Choice After Testing Them All (2026)
An online course platform is cloud software that lets you build, sell, and deliver educational content in the form of structured multi-media online courses.
Thinkific is the best online platform to sell courses, in my experience. It offers detailed course creation tools, a great student experience, and built-in marketing features that actually work (all at a reasonable price)
But there are other top-notch platforms too, depending on what you need: 
✅ Kajabi – Best all-in-one platform with excellent course creation and advanced email marketing features.
✅ LearnWorlds – Best for interactive learning with robust AI features and SCORM support. 
✅ LearnDash – WordPress LMS, gives you full control over your course site. 
✅ Circle – The best platform for building an online community + sell courses. 
I’ve broken down the strengths and weaknesses of each tool in the full article—plus, check out the 200+ comments where other creators share their insights and questions

I’ve been launching online courses, helping professionals sell their expertise, and consulting organizations on training and development for over 20 years.
As a part of my consulting work at Tagoras and Learning Revolution, I get to use every online course platform in the market.
So if you’re looking for an online platform for selling courses and have no idea where to start, trust me on this.
In my experience, Thinkific is the most user-friendly and robust platform for both beginners and experienced course sellers.
It gives you:
- A powerful multimedia course builder
- The ability to host live sessions
- A drag-and-drop website builder to sell your courses
- Built-in email marketing, automation, and sales funnels
- Seamless payment integration
- Support for memberships, communities, and digital downloads
I’ve tested nearly every major course selling platform, and Thinkific is the one I recommend to anyone who wants to focus on content, while the platform takes care of everything else.
But it’s not perfect for everyone. That’s why I’ve also included other platforms I admire and have personally used over the years.
Each one shines in its own way, depending on your goals, teaching style, budget, and audience. There’s no one-size-fits-all.
Especially, with AI advancements in full swing, 2026 promises to be a huge year for the online course industry because of how platforms are evolving with these changes.
What follows is my honest breakdown of the best online course platforms, what they do well, where they fall short, and who they’re best suited for.
I’ve rechecked and updated the pricing & features of all platforms in this article for 2026 to give you the latest insights

The Best Online Course Platforms For Sellers Compared – An Overview
Instead of overwhelming you with a giant list of 20+ online course platforms, I’ve handpicked the ones that actually deliver.
There are plenty of others out there. But in my experience, they just don’t measure up enough to make the cut right now.
These are the best platforms and software for selling online courses.
I’ll go into the details shortly, but here’s a quick snapshot of what each online course platform offers:
Platform	Best for	Starting Price 2026 (monthly billing)
Thinkific	Excellent course features and built-in email marketing	$49/month
Kajabi	All-in-one platform for courses, funnels, and email	$89/month
Mighty Networks	For courses + community	$129/month
LearnWorlds	Structured learning paths, SCORM, and interactive tools	$29/month
Kartra	All-in-one platform with strong marketing features	$59/month
LearnDash	To turn your WordPress site into a full LMS	$199/year (plugin) or $39/month (hosted)
Podia	Simple, user-friendly courses and email marketing	$39/month
Teachable	Simplest course setup, negligible marketing features 	$39/month
Circle	The best platform to build an online community + sell courses	$89/month
Udemy	Instructors who want to teach without worrying about tech or marketing	Free (revenue share applies)

Online Course Platform Selection Tool
Simply tell us your preferences and this tool will help you find the perfect online course platform for your goals.

The Top Online Course Software For Teachers & Sellers [Updated Pricing & Features 2026]
I get to use different online course platforms regularly because I work with clients across industries and from all kinds of backgrounds. 
So the opinions I’m sharing here are based on real-world experience, not just product demos or affiliate pitches.
All of these tools are solid. Honestly, there’s no wrong choice.
But each one comes with its own strengths and trade-offs. And understanding those differences is key to choosing a platform that actually fits your online learning goals, your content, and your workflow.
Let’s break them down one by one.

1. Thinkific (Top Pick)
- Main features: Multimedia course builder, email marketing, live sessions, memberships
- Best for: Creators who want a complete course platform without tech headaches
- Starting price: $49/month
Thinkific is my top choice as the best online course platform for most professionals.
It is a full-featured platform for online courses that lets you build structured learning experiences, sell coaching, offer subscriptions, host communities, and sell digital products—all from one place. 
It also comes with built-in email marketing, automation, and AI tools that help you grow your audience and make more sales.
I recommend Thinkific to most of my clients, especially if you want to stay focused on your content and not get stuck with tech headaches. 
Phil Hynes’ story is a great example of how Thinkific empowers non-technical users to monetize their expertise.
Phil runs a fitness membership program and an online course hosted on Thinkific. Collectively, these programs help him earn over six-figures a year, serving thousands of customers worldwide.
Thinkific’s biggest strength is its course creation features. You can build multi-layered courses with modules, lessons, PDFs, videos, quizzes, assignments, and even prerequisites or drip content. 
You also get progress tracking, certificates, and surveys, giving you everything you need to structure a real learning experience.
Thinkific also has a clean AI-powered drag-and-drop website and landing page builder that comes with customizable templates. 
On the engagement side, Thinkific lets you build private communities under your brand, connect course discussions to lessons, and even host live events. Some creators use it just to run memberships and community programs.
What really makes Thinkific stand out now is its native email marketing. You can run sales emails, segment audiences, and even send abandoned cart reminders without paying for another tool.
It also handles payments globally, including payment plans and subscriptions, and now offers Buy Now, Pay Later options like Klarna and Afterpay.
A big vote of confidence for Thinkific is bestselling author Dorie Clark – who has been a guest on the Learning Revolution podcast and is generally a brilliant and business-savvy person – has used it for her online courses.

Thinkific Features
Here’s a quick overview of the platform’s standout capabilities:
- Unlimited Courses – No caps on how many courses you can publish, even on the Basic plan.
- Drag-and-Drop Course Builder – Easily structure your content without needing to code.
- Live Lessons & Webinars – Host Zoom sessions or webinars directly from your course interface.
- Digital Downloads – Sell or offer downloadable resources like PDFs, worksheets, or guides.
- Memberships – Create recurring revenue with member-only content and access tiers.
- Communities – Build student communities for engagement and discussion.
- Email Marketing & Automation – Capture leads, send automated AI-generated campaigns, abandoned-cart reminders, and integrate with Mailchimp/Zapier
- Bundles & Add-ons – Upsell with course bundles, bonus content, or product add-ons.
- Group Orders & Invoicing – Sell access to businesses or teams and handle B2B payments.
- Analytics & Reporting – Track sales, student progress, and engagement with Thinkific Analytics.
- Branded Experience – Remove Thinkific branding on Grow plan and above.
- SCORM Support – Use SCORM content on Plus plan for more interactive learning.
- Student Learning Paths – Create guided learning journeys on Thinkific Plus.

Thinkific Pricing And Free Trial: How Much Does It Cost?
Thinkific no longer offers a free plan, but you can start with a free trial of any paid plan—no credit card required.
Here’s a breakdown of the paid plans:
- Basic – $36/mo (annual) – Great for getting started with essential course delivery tools.
- Start – $74/mo (annual) – Best for creators ready to scale with unlimited memberships and live lessons.
- Grow – $149/mo (annual) – Built for pros who want advanced features, better support, and full white-labeling.
- Thinkific Plus – Custom – Tailored for high-growth teams needing SCORM, SSO, and enterprise integrations.
All plans offer 25% savings when billed annually.
Plan	Communities	Downloads & Coaching	Memberships & Bundles	Branding	Advanced Features
Basic	1	5 each	❌	❌	❌
Start	1	Unlimited	✅	❌	HTML/CSS Editing
Grow	3	Unlimited	✅	✅	Group Orders, Analytics, API
Plus	Unlimited	Unlimited	✅	✅	SCORM, SSO, CRM, Learning Paths

To go deeper, here’s our full Thinkific review.


2. Kajabi – The Best All-In-One Platform
- Main features: Courses & memberships, advanced email marketing automation, funnels
- Best for: Managing your whole creator business with one platform
- Starting price: $89/month
When I say Kajabi is an all-in-one platform, I mean it has everything you need to build, market, and sell online courses or any other digital products, all in one place. 
More than just a course creator, Kajabi is your website builder, email marketing and newsletter tool, payment processor, and more, designed to help you focus on growing your business instead of juggling multiple platforms.
You can call it a complete audience-building and monetization platform.
In 2024, we sent Jackie, one of our team members, to Kajabi Hero Live to see what it’s like to use Kajabi in real life. 
She came back blown away. Meeting creators like Justin Welsh and Graham Cochrane, who’ve built thriving businesses using Kajabi, gave us a firsthand look at what’s possible. 
Hearing their stories was inspiring and proved how much Kajabi empowers creators to transform their knowledge into a sustainable business.
Kajabi lets you create everything from courses and memberships to coaching programs and digital downloads. 
You can build beautiful websites and landing pages, set up marketing funnels to bring in leads, and even automate email campaigns to nurture your audience. 
It’s the kind of platform that grows with you. Whether you’re just starting out or scaling to the next level, it eliminates the need for piecing together different tools.
If you want to spend less time dealing with tech headaches and more time creating great content and connecting with your audience, Kajabi gives you the tools to make it happen. 
However, since it replaces several tools in your tech stack, it’s at a higher price point than your standard course platform. Which means it might not be a great fit if you’re on a tight budget.

Kajabi Features
These are the core, product-defining features that make Kajabi a powerful all-in-one platform:
- Website Builder – Create a fully branded website with customizable templates.
- Course & Membership Creation – Build and sell online courses, memberships, and coaching programs.
- Marketing Funnels – Design automated sales funnels to convert leads into customers.
- Email Marketing – Send newsletters, automated sequences, and targeted campaigns.
- Landing Pages – Create high-converting landing pages for your offers.
- Checkout & Payments – Accept payments, set up subscriptions, and manage transactions.
- Analytics – Track your sales, traffic, and customer behavior.
- Community – Build a community space for your audience to connect.
- Mobile App – Offer a branded mobile app for your courses and community.
- AI Tools – Use AI to help with content creation, marketing copy, and more.

Kajabi Pricing
Kajabi offers three main plans, all of which include a 30-day free trial:
- Basic – $89/month (billed annually) – Perfect for getting started with one website and three products.
- Growth – $199/month (billed annually) – Best for scaling with 10 products, advanced automations, and affiliate management.
- Pro – $319/month (billed annually) – For established businesses needing 25 products, custom code, and priority support.
All plans include unlimited landing pages, marketing emails, and a website. The higher tiers unlock more products, admin users, and advanced features like Kajabi AI and custom code editing.


3. Mighty Networks – Best For Courses + Community
- Main features: Community-first platform, courses, memberships, events
- Best for: Creators who want community at the core of their business
- Starting price: $129/month
Mighty Networks is built around the idea that community drives engagement and retention. It’s not just a course platform—it’s a space where your audience can connect, discuss, and learn together.
If you believe that the real value of your course comes from the people in it and the interactions they have, Mighty Networks might be your best bet.
The platform lets you host courses, memberships, and events—all within a community environment. You can create subgroups, host live streams, and even offer a branded mobile app so your members can engage on the go.
What sets Mighty Networks apart is how it blends content and conversation. Instead of a traditional course player, your lessons live alongside discussion threads, member profiles, and event calendars. This creates a more immersive and social learning experience.
I’ve seen this work incredibly well for creators in niches like wellness, personal development, and professional networking, where peer support and accountability are key.
The downside? It’s pricier than some standalone course platforms, and if you don’t plan to leverage the community features, you might be paying for tools you won’t use.

Mighty Networks Features
- Community Spaces – Create public or private communities with subgroups and topics.
- Courses & Memberships – Deliver structured content alongside community discussions.
- Live Streaming & Events – Host live video sessions, workshops, and calendar events.
- Mobile App – Offer a custom-branded iOS and Android app for your community.
- Member Profiles – Let members connect and network with each other.
- Analytics – Track engagement, growth, and revenue.
- Zapier Integrations – Connect with other tools in your stack.

Mighty Networks Pricing
- Community Plan – $129/month (billed annually) – Includes one community, courses, and basic features.
- Business Plan – $299/month (billed annually) – Adds a branded mobile app, advanced analytics, and more admin seats.
- Mighty Pro – Custom pricing – A white-label solution for larger organizations and influencers.


4. LearnWorlds – Best For Interactive Learning & SCORM
- Main features: Interactive video, SCORM support, AI tools, assessments
- Best for: Educators and trainers who need advanced learning tools
- Starting price: $29/month
LearnWorlds is built for creating engaging, interactive learning experiences. It goes beyond standard video-and-PDF courses by offering tools like interactive video players, built-in ebook creators, and robust assessment features.
If you’re teaching a subject that requires hands-on practice, quizzes, or certifications, LearnWorlds gives you the tools to make it happen. It also supports SCORM, which is essential if you’re working with corporate clients or existing e-learning content.
The platform has invested heavily in AI features, including an AI assistant that can help you generate course outlines, quiz questions, and even video scripts. This can be a huge time-saver when you’re in the content creation phase.
LearnWorlds also offers a beautiful, customizable course player and a website builder, so you can create a fully branded learning environment.
The trade-off? The interface can feel a bit more complex than platforms like Thinkific or Teachable, especially if you’re new to course creation. But if you need advanced learning features, it’s worth the learning curve.

LearnWorlds Features
- Interactive Video – Add clickable hotspots, quizzes, and chapters to your videos.
- SCORM & xAPI Support – Import and track existing e-learning content.
- AI Assistant – Generate content, quizzes, and outlines with AI.
- Assessment Engine – Create quizzes, exams, and assignments with advanced grading.
- Ebook Creator – Build interactive ebooks within the platform.
- Certificates – Issue customizable certificates upon course completion.
- White-Labeling – Fully brand your school, including the mobile app.

LearnWorlds Pricing
- Starter – $29/month (billed annually) – For one admin and basic course features.
- Pro Trainer – $99/month (billed annually) – Adds unlimited courses, advanced branding, and affiliate marketing.
- Learning Center – $299/month (billed annually) – Includes custom domain, SSO, and bulk actions.
- High Volume & Corporate – Custom pricing – For large-scale or enterprise needs.


5. Kartra – All-In-One Platform With Strong Marketing
- Main features: Marketing funnels, email automation, helpdesk, membership sites
- Best for: Marketers who want powerful automation alongside their courses
- Starting price: $59/month
Kartra is another all-in-one platform that combines course creation with a full suite of marketing and business tools. It’s similar to Kajabi in its approach but often appeals to users who want more granular control over their marketing automation.
With Kartra, you can build sales funnels, create membership sites, send email broadcasts and sequences, run helpdesk ticketing, and even host a calendar for scheduling—all from one dashboard.
Its strength lies in its marketing capabilities. The funnel builder is visual and flexible, and the email automation includes behavioral triggers (like tagging users who click a specific link). If you’re focused on converting leads and maximizing customer lifetime value, Kartra gives you the tools to do it.
The course builder is solid, though not as educationally focused as LearnWorlds or Thinkific. It’s more about delivering content as part of a broader customer journey.
Kartra’s pricing is mid-range, making it more accessible than Kajabi for some users. However, the interface can feel busy, and there’s a learning curve to mastering all its features.

Kartra Features
- Funnel Builder – Create sales, webinar, and launch funnels.
- Email Marketing – Send broadcasts, automations, and behavioral-based sequences.
- Membership Sites – Host courses and gated content.
- Helpdesk – Manage customer support tickets.
- Calendar – Schedule appointments and meetings.
- Forms & Surveys – Capture leads and gather feedback.
- Affiliate Management – Run an affiliate program for your products.

Kartra Pricing
- Starter – $59/month (billed annually) – Includes 2,500 leads, 15,000 emails/month, and 1 domain.
- Growth – $119/month (billed annually) – 12,500 leads, unlimited emails, and 3 domains.
- Professional – $229/month (billed annually) – 25,000 leads, 5 domains, and advanced features.
- Enterprise – $549/month (billed annually) – 50,000 leads, 10 domains, and premium support.


6. LearnDash – Best WordPress LMS Plugin
- Main features: WordPress plugin, full control, extensive integrations
- Best for: WordPress users who want complete ownership and flexibility
- Starting price: $199/year (plugin) or $39/month (hosted)
LearnDash is a WordPress plugin that turns your existing WordPress site into a full-featured learning management system (LMS). If you already have a WordPress website and want to keep everything under one roof, LearnDash is a powerful choice.
The biggest advantage of LearnDash is control. You own your site, your data, and your content. You can customize every aspect of your course experience using WordPress themes and plugins. You’re also not tied to a monthly subscription for the core platform (though you’ll need hosting and may pay for additional plugins).
LearnDash is feature-rich, offering drip content, quizzes, assignments, certificates, and advanced user roles. It integrates with popular page builders like Elementor and Thrive Architect, as well as membership plugins and email marketing services.
The downside? You’re responsible for your own hosting, security, updates, and tech support. If you’re not comfortable managing a WordPress site, the learning curve and maintenance might be a burden.
For those who are, LearnDash offers unparalleled flexibility and is a cost-effective solution in the long run.

LearnDash Features
- Course Builder – Create courses with lessons, topics, quizzes, and assignments.
- Drip-Feed Content – Release content on a schedule.
- Advanced Quizzing – Build quizzes with multiple question types and timers.
- Certificates – Issue custom certificates for course completion.
- Integrations – Connect with WooCommerce, membership plugins, and page builders.
- User Roles – Assign instructors, group leaders, and students.
- Reporting – Track student progress and engagement.

LearnDash Pricing
- LearnDash Plugin – $199/year for a single site license. Includes one year of updates and support.
- LearnDash Cloud – $39/month (billed annually). A hosted solution that includes WordPress hosting, themes, and the LearnDash plugin.


7. Podia – Simple & User-Friendly
- Main features: Courses, digital downloads, memberships, email marketing
- Best for: Beginners and creators who value simplicity
- Starting price: $39/month
Podia is all about simplicity. It’s designed to be intuitive and easy to use, making it a great choice if you’re new to selling online courses or if you just want a platform that gets out of your way.
You can sell courses, digital downloads, memberships, and coaching—all from one clean dashboard. Podia also includes built-in email marketing, so you can communicate with your customers without needing another tool.
The platform doesn’t have the advanced features of Kajabi or the community focus of Mighty Networks, but it covers the essentials well. The course builder is straightforward, the checkout process is smooth, and the customer support is highly regarded.
Podia’s pricing is also straightforward, with no transaction fees on any plan. This makes it easy to understand exactly what you’ll pay each month.
If you want a no-fuss platform to start selling quickly, Podia is a strong contender. Just know that if your needs become more complex, you might eventually outgrow it.

Podia Features
- Course Creation – Build simple courses with videos, text, and files.
- Digital Downloads – Sell ebooks, templates, and other files.
- Memberships – Create subscription-based access to content.
- Email Marketing – Send broadcasts and automated sequences to your customers.
- Website Builder – Create a simple website with a few pages.
- Affiliate Marketing – Run an affiliate program for your products.
- Zero Transaction Fees – Keep all your sales revenue.

Podia Pricing
- Starter – $39/month – For one product and basic features.
- Mover – $89/month – Includes unlimited products, memberships, and email marketing.
- Shaker – $199/month – Adds advanced features like third-party code and affiliate payouts.


8. Teachable – The Simplest Course Setup
- Main features: Easy course builder, student management, basic marketing
- Best for: Instructors who want the fastest path to a published course
- Starting price: $39/month
Teachable is one of the original dedicated course platforms, and it’s known for its simplicity. The course builder is incredibly easy to use—you can literally drag and drop your videos and files to create lessons.
It handles all the technical details like video hosting, payment processing, and student management, so you can focus on teaching. Teachable also has a large user base and a wealth of community knowledge, which can be helpful for beginners.
However, in recent years, Teachable has lagged behind competitors in terms of built-in marketing and business tools. Its email marketing features are basic, and it doesn’t offer native funnel builders or advanced automation. You’ll likely need to integrate with other tools as you grow.
Teachable also charges transaction fees on its lower-tier plans, which can eat into your profits.
If your primary goal is to get a course online as quickly and easily as possible, and you’re okay with using other tools for marketing, Teachable is a solid option.

Teachable Features
- Simple Course Builder – Drag-and-drop interface for creating lessons.
- Integrated Payments – Accept payments via Stripe and PayPal.
- Student Management – Enroll students, track progress, and issue certificates.
- Basic Quizzes – Create simple graded quizzes.
- Coupons & Upsells – Offer discounts and one-click upsells.
- Affiliate Marketing – Run an affiliate program (on higher plans).

Teachable Pricing
- Free Plan – $0/month with 1 product and 10% transaction fee.
- Basic – $39/month (billed annually) – 5% transaction fee.
- Pro – $119/month (billed annually) – 0% transaction fee, includes advanced features.
- Pro+ – $199/month (billed annually) – Adds bulk student enrollment and custom user roles.


9. Circle – Best Community Platform That Also Sells Courses
- Main features: Community spaces, courses, events, member profiles
- Best for: Creators who want community first, with courses as a feature
- Starting price: $89/month
Circle is primarily a community platform, but it has added robust course features over time. If building a thriving, engaged community is your top priority, and you want to offer courses within that community, Circle is an excellent choice.
The platform is designed for discussion, connection, and events. You can create spaces for different topics, host live streams, and foster conversations among members. The course features allow you to structure learning paths with videos, text, and discussions attached to each lesson.
This integration means your course content lives right alongside the community conversation about it, which can boost engagement and completion rates.
Circle is less of a traditional “course platform” and more of a community hub with learning capabilities. If you’re looking for advanced course creation tools like SCORM or intricate quizzes, you might find it limiting. But for community-driven learning, it’s hard to beat.

Circle Features
- Community Spaces – Organize discussions into categories and subcategories.
- Courses – Create structured learning paths with multimedia content.
- Live Events – Host video calls, streams, and scheduled events.
- Member Directory – Help members network and connect.
- Mobile App – Native iOS and Android apps for community access.
- Integrations – Connect with Zoom, Slack, and other tools.

Circle Pricing
- Basic – $89/month (billed annually) – Includes one community, courses, and basic features.
- Professional – $199/month (billed annually) – Adds multiple communities, advanced analytics, and API access.
- Enterprise – Custom pricing – For large organizations with custom needs.


10. Udemy – The Marketplace Platform
- Main features: Massive built-in audience, course hosting, reviews
- Best for: Instructors who want to teach without handling marketing or tech
- Starting price: Free (revenue share applies)
Udemy is a marketplace, not a platform you own. You create your course once, and Udemy hosts it, markets it, and handles all sales and customer service. In return, they take a significant revenue share (often 50% or more).
The huge advantage is access to Udemy’s millions of students. If you can create a course on a popular topic and optimize it for Udemy’s search algorithm, you can reach an audience that would be very difficult to build on your own.
The downside is lack of control. You don’t own the customer relationship, you can’t set your own price during frequent Udemy sales, and your income is unpredictable. It’s also difficult to stand out among thousands of other courses.
Udemy is best seen as a marketing channel, not a business foundation. Many successful instructors use Udemy to build their reputation and then funnel students to their own website or community where they can sell higher-ticket offerings.

Udemy Features
- Course Hosting – Upload your videos, quizzes, and resources.
- Built-in Audience – Access to millions of potential students.
- Marketing & Promotions – Udemy runs site-wide sales and promotions.
- Reviews & Ratings – Social proof from student feedback.
- Instructor Support – Resources and community for instructors.

Udemy Pricing
It’s free to publish a course on Udemy. Udemy sets the price and runs frequent sales. Instructors earn revenue based on a share model:
- Instructor Promotion: If you bring the student via your own link, you earn 97% of the revenue.
- Udemy Organic: If Udemy brings the student, you typically earn 37% of the revenue.
- Udemy Deal: If the student comes via a Udemy-initiated promotion, you earn 25% of the revenue.

How To Choose The Best Online Course Platform For You
With so many options, how do you pick the right one? Don’t just look at features and price. Think about your own skills, your business goals, and your students’ experience.
Here are the key questions to ask yourself:
1. What’s your primary goal? (Selling courses, building a community, creating an all-in-one business?)
2. What’s your technical comfort level? (Do you want everything handled, or do you enjoy tinkering with tech?)
3. What’s your budget? (Consider both monthly fees and potential transaction fees.)
4. How important are marketing tools? (Do you need built-in email and funnels, or will you use separate tools?)
5. What kind of learning experience do you want to create? (Simple video delivery, interactive lessons, community-driven?)
My general advice:
- If you want the best balance of power and ease-of-use: Choose Thinkific.
- If you want an all-in-one business platform and budget is less of a concern: Choose Kajabi.
- If community is central to your offering: Choose Mighty Networks or Circle.
- If you need advanced learning features (SCORM, interactive video): Choose LearnWorlds.
- If you already use and love WordPress: Choose LearnDash.
- If you want the simplest, fastest setup: Choose Podia or Teachable.
- If you want to reach a massive audience without doing marketing: Consider Udemy (as a supplement).
Remember, you can always start with one platform and migrate later if your needs change. The most important thing is to start creating and sharing your knowledge.

Final Thoughts
Choosing the right online course platform is a big decision, but it doesn’t have to be paralyzing. All the platforms on this list are capable of helping you build a successful online teaching business.
My top recommendation for most creators in 2026 is still Thinkific. It offers the best combination of a powerful course builder, essential business tools (like email marketing), and a reasonable price. It grows with you from your first course to a full-fledged online school.
But the “best” platform is the one that best fits your specific needs, teaching style, and goals. Use the comparisons and insights in this article to make an informed choice.
The online course industry is more vibrant than ever, and the tools available to creators are incredibly powerful. Don’t let platform paralysis stop you from sharing what you know. Pick one, start building, and adjust as you learn.
Got questions about a specific platform or your situation? Leave a comment below—I read and respond to them regularly.
with: In my experience, Thinkific is the most user-friendly and robust platform for both beginners and experienced course sellers.
Thinkific’s biggest strength is its course creation features. You can build multi-layered courses with modules, lessons, PDFs, videos, quizzes, assignments, and even prerequisites or drip content.
If you want to spend less time dealing with tech headaches and more time creating great content and connecting with your audience, Kajabi gives you the tools to make it happen.
